inclusion_criteria: Adults (≥18 years) from the general population; mixed-age studies eligible if adult data are separable.
Comparative human studies (RCTs, quasi-experiments, cohort, or case-control) contrasting sodium intake levels or sodium-reduction interventions with a higher/usual intake control.
Lower sodium group or intervention targets consistent with ≤2 g/day sodium (≤5 g/day salt) or clearly lower than comparator; comparator is higher/usual or above-recommended intake.

Reports at least one eligible outcome: blood pressure, cardiovascular disease events/mortality, physical performance, composite renal outcomes or chronic kidney disease, osteoporosis/fracture, or all-cause mortality.
exclusion_criteria: Studies limited to children/adolescents, pregnant-only cohorts, or narrowly defined patient-only clinical populations (e.g., dialysis-only, heart failure-only) without a general adult sample.
Non-comparative designs (single-arm, cross-sectional without intake groups), case series/reports, editorials, or reviews; studies lacking a clear lower vs higher sodium contrast.
Do not report any eligible outcome or provide insufficient/extractable outcome data for adults.
Multi-component interventions where the specific effect of sodium reduction cannot be isolated (e.g., bundled diet programs without separate sodium analysis).
screening_criteria.description: Screen comparative studies in general adult populations that contrast lower vs higher/usual sodium intake (≤2 g/day sodium or ≤5 g/day salt) and report prespecified cardiometabolic, renal, skeletal, performance, or mortality outcomes.

included_articles.title: Effects of Reduced Dietary Sodium and the DASH Diet on GFR: The DASH-Sodium Trial.
